topic: Building multi-tenant leave management for GCC labour law
post:
Leave management sounds simple until you build it for GCC labour law.

Annual leave accrual in Oman: 30 calendar days after one year, pro-rated for partial years, with a specific rule about whether weekends during leave count.

Sick leave: 10 days full pay, 20 days half pay, 30 days no pay — but the entitlement resets annually and the clock runs differently during probation.

Maternity: 50 days fully paid. Paternity: 6 days. Emergency leave: no statutory minimum, handled by employer policy.

Now multiply this by a multi-tenant setup where each client company has slightly different policies layered on top of the statutory minimums.

The naive approach: store leave rules per tenant as configuration. This works until a company changes their policy mid-year and you need to recalculate historical accruals.

The right approach: version-controlled policy objects with effective dates. Every leave transaction references the policy version active on that date. Changes are prospective, not retroactive — unless explicitly overridden.

We built this wrong the first time. The recalculation problem hit us 4 months in when our first client updated their annual leave policy.

Build the version history from day one. Retrofitting it is painful.

#BuildInPublic #SaaS #HRTech #GCC #SmartPROHub
